name: CreatorKit
one_liner: Linktree-style monetization hub for India's creators (paid links, tips via UPI, mini-storefronts).
us_signal: Linktree/Beacons reached tens of millions of users; creator-economy tooling is a proven category.

rationale.tam: India has 100M+ creators; the monetizing subset is smaller but growing fast.
rationale.demand: Creators already pay for link/tip/store tools globally.
rationale.competition: Some local players, but the space is fragmented and not locked up.
rationale.feasibility: A link-in-bio + UPI tips MVP is genuinely shippable in weeks.
rationale.reg_lightness: No licensing; UPI payouts via a PSP keep compliance light.
rationale.distribution: Creators self-distribute the product to their own audiences — viral by design.
rationale.monetization: Low ARPU; relies on volume + a take-rate on tips/sales.
rationale.moat: Thin — differentiation is taste, speed, and India-native payouts.

india_fit: Strong: UPI tips and ₹ micro-pricing fit creator behavior; Hindi + regional language UI matters; distribution rides the creators themselves via Instagram/YouTube.

name: ShopWhats
one_liner: WhatsApp-first storefront builder for kirana and small retailers (catalog, orders, UPI checkout).
us_signal: Shopify proved storefront SaaS; the India twist is WhatsApp-native commerce.

rationale.tam: Tens of millions of small retailers; huge and underserved.
rationale.demand: SMBs already sell over WhatsApp manually — clear pull.
rationale.competition: Dukaan/Bikayi and others are well-funded incumbents — real competition.
rationale.feasibility: Catalog + order + payment link MVP is achievable for a small team.
rationale.reg_lightness: Mostly light; GST invoicing adds some work but no licensing.
rationale.distribution: WhatsApp Business + word-of-mouth among shopkeepers is cheap and fast.
rationale.monetization: Price sensitivity is high; ₹ pricing must be very low.
rationale.moat: Network effects per merchant's customers, but switching costs are modest.

india_fit: Excellent: WhatsApp is the de-facto commerce channel; UPI checkout + ₹ pricing essential; regional languages a must. Main flag: strong funded incumbents.

name: InvoiceFlow
one_liner: GST-native invoicing + payment-reminder SaaS for Indian freelancers and SMBs.
us_signal: FreshBooks/Bill.com validated SMB invoicing; recurring, sticky revenue.

rationale.tam: Millions of GST-registered SMBs and freelancers.
rationale.demand: Invoicing + getting paid is a real, recurring pain people pay to solve.
rationale.competition: Zoho, Khatabook, Vyapar are entrenched — tough field.
rationale.feasibility: Well-understood domain; MVP is buildable, GST adds scope.
rationale.reg_lightness: GST e-invoicing compliance is moderate, manageable work.
rationale.distribution: SEO + CA/accountant referral channels are automatable.
rationale.monetization: Clear willingness to pay; subscription fits.
rationale.moat: Data lock-in over time, but incumbents already have it.

india_fit: Good: GST e-invoicing is the localization core; UPI payment reminders are a natural feature; ₹ subscription pricing. Main flag: crowded incumbent field.

name: DocketAI
one_liner: Document automation + matter management for India's small law firms.
us_signal: Clio and US legal SaaS proved small-firm willingness to pay for practice tools.

rationale.tam: Niche but real; many small firms, lower digitization.
rationale.demand: Demand exists but adoption is slow and relationship-driven.
rationale.competition: Few strong India-specific incumbents — a genuine gap.
rationale.feasibility: Doc templates + AI drafting is feasible; integrations add work.
rationale.reg_lightness: Client-data sensitivity (DPDP) adds care but no licensing.
rationale.distribution: Bar associations + referrals; harder to automate cheaply.
rationale.monetization: Professionals pay for time-savings — decent ARPU.
rationale.moat: Workflow lock-in + India legal templates create some durability.

india_fit: Moderate: needs India legal templates and language support; DPDP-compliant data handling; distribution is relationship-heavy, which slows a solo founder.

name: LendBuddy
one_liner: BNPL / micro-credit app for India's thin-file consumers (an Affirm-style model).
us_signal: Affirm/Klarna proved massive BNPL demand and revenue.

rationale.demand: Clear consumer demand for short-term credit.
rationale.competition: Several players, space is contested but not closed.
rationale.feasibility: NOT solo-feasible: needs lending licence, capital, underwriting, collections.
rationale.reg_lightness: Heaviest possible — RBI/NBFC rules, digital-lending guidelines.
rationale.monetization: Strong unit economics IF you can lend legally.
rationale.moat: Data + balance sheet — neither available to a solo founder early.

india_fit: Poor for a solo founder despite the huge market: RBI digital-lending regulation and capital requirements make this infeasible to MVP alone. Included to show the rubric correctly penalizes regulatory/feasibility load.

name: HealthLog
one_liner: Personal health-record + medication tracker app for Indian patients and caregivers.
us_signal: US health-tracking apps show engagement, though monetization is hard.

rationale.demand: Interest is real but willingness to pay is weak.
rationale.feasibility: Health-data handling and integrations are heavy for a solo team.
rationale.reg_lightness: Health data is highly sensitive under DPDP — heavy compliance.
rationale.monetization: Consumers rarely pay for health trackers directly.
rationale.moat: Weak — data sensitivity raises liability without a clear moat.

india_fit: Weak: health-data sensitivity under the DPDP Act is a serious compliance burden, monetization is unproven, and there's no cheap distribution channel.
